name: Federal Pell Grant
description: The largest federal need-based grant program for undergraduate students. Award amount is determined by Student Aid Index (SAI), cost of attendance, enrollment status, and program length.
eligibility: Undergraduate students with exceptional financial need who have not earned a bachelor's or professional degree. Must file FAFSA and have SAI below the maximum threshold (typically around -1500 to 6206 for full award).
award_amount: Up to $7,395 per academic year (2024-2025). Actual award depends on SAI, enrollment intensity, and cost of attendance. Minimum scheduled award is approximately $740.
application_process: Automatic consideration upon filing FAFSA. No separate application required. Award letter provided by each college's financial aid office.
timeline: Awarded per academic year. Lifetime eligibility limited to 12 semesters (or equivalent) of full-time enrollment.
advisor_notes: Pell is foundational — maximize by ensuring accurate income reporting. Students with incarcerated parent or unusual circumstances may qualify for special calculations. Check if college participates in Year-Round Pell for summer enrollment.
